title: pseudo-fumaria alba
intro: Pseudo-fumaria alba, also known as white false fumewort, is a flowering plant species in the borage family, characterized by its white or pale yellowish flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids like pseudo-fumaline and fumaline, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. Pseudo-fumaria alba has been associated with various health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential use in managing digestive problems and menstrual disorders. Herbal preparations of pseudo-fumaria alba can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional due to potential side effects and interactions.
